turns.text: I have a blank canvas and a feeling of vertigo. What should I paint?
Not a landscape. Something that feels like falling, but upwards.
Yes! A flock of origami cranes, their paper edges glowing, ascending through a cracked clock face.
The clock's numbers are melting into puddles of mercury on the ground below.
And from each puddle, a tiny, perfect silver tree is growing. The roots are clockwork.
Title it 'The Uphill Flight of Forgotten Seconds'. I'll start mixing the blues.
I'm trying to write a story about a sentient lighthouse. Where should I start?
I was thinking its light could be its consciousness, pulsing with its emotions.
Maybe a storm is approaching, and the lighthouse feels a deep dread, making its beam falter.
A lone sailor on a small boat sees the inconsistent light and senses something is wrong, not just with the weather.
The lighthouse wants to warn the sailor but can only communicate through patterns of light and shadow on the cliffs.
I think the climax will be the lighthouse sacrificing its own lens, focusing all its light into one desperate, guiding flash to save the sailor.
Hi, I'd like to open a new savings account.
What are the current interest rates you offer?
Is there a minimum deposit required to open the account?
Are there any monthly maintenance fees?
How can I access my account online?
Thank you, that answers all my questions for now.
I'm looking to open a new savings account. What are your current interest rates?
Can you explain the difference between a high-yield savings account and a regular one?
What is the minimum balance required to avoid monthly fees on your premium savings product?
How often is interest compounded and paid on the high-yield option?
Are there any withdrawal limits or penalties I should be aware of?
What documents do I need to provide to open an account online?
I've been having a persistent headache for three days now.
It's a throbbing pain on the right side of my head.
I haven't taken any medication for it yet.
No, I haven't noticed any vision changes or nausea.
My sleep has been normal, about seven hours a night.
Yes, I have a history of migraines in my family.
I've had a persistent headache and fever for three days now.
I also feel very tired and have some body aches.
I haven't taken my temperature, but I definitely feel warm.
No, I haven't had any nausea or changes in my vision.
I tried some over-the-counter pain reliever yesterday, but it only helped for a few hours.
Okay, I will schedule an appointment with my doctor right away. Thank you.
I've been having a persistent headache for three days now.
It's a throbbing pain on the right side of my head, and light seems to make it worse.
I haven't had any head injuries recently, but I have been under a lot of stress at work.
I took some over-the-counter pain relievers, but they only helped for a few hours.
I don't have a fever or nausea, but my vision has felt a little blurry at times.
Should I come in for an appointment, or is there something else I can try first?
I've been feeling really down lately, like nothing matters.
It's been hard to get out of bed or find motivation for anything.
I keep thinking I'm a burden to my friends and family.
Sometimes I just feel completely empty inside.
I don't really enjoy the things I used to love doing.
Is it normal to feel this way for weeks at a time?
I've been feeling really low and unmotivated for weeks now.
I just can't seem to get out of bed or find joy in anything I used to love.
My sleep is terrible, and I'm either not eating or eating too much junk.
I feel like I'm a burden to my friends and family when I talk about this.
Is it normal to have these thoughts of just wanting to disappear sometimes?
I think I need help, but I'm scared of what that means or where to even start.
I've been feeling really low and unmotivated for weeks now.
I just can't seem to get out of bed most mornings, and everything feels pointless.
I've stopped seeing my friends, and even my hobbies don't bring me joy anymore.
I know I should probably talk to someone, but the thought of it is overwhelming.
Sometimes I wonder if I'll ever feel like my old self again.
I think I need help, but I don't know where to start.
